name: H.I.R.A Growth Log
subtitle: How Herwanto Intelligent Response Agent grew from an installable assistant into a more reliable, visually coherent personal operating system.

summary.currentStage: Wordsmith semantic layer with clarification memory, nuance-aware routing, and safer action intent

metrics.label: Days of build acceleration
metrics.note: From first installable PWA commit to a sharper Nothing-style app identity with explicit trust rails, ClassOps intelligence, Postgres-backed operational memory, OpenAI-first model routing, and wordsmith-grade semantic intent.

metrics.label: Tracked upgrade commits
metrics.note: Grouped from git history through the growth-log minisite.

metrics.label: Memory buckets
metrics.note: Profile, preferences, teaching, business, sports, topic profiles, corrections, reflections, source notes, and more, now Postgres-backed with priority-weighted prompt injection.

metrics.label: Specialist live domains
metrics.note: Weather, MUIS, Gmail, Liverpool/F1, documents, classlists, and web links.

capabilityRings.name: Interface
capabilityRings.description: Installable PWA, minimal Nothing-style home-screen icon, restored cockpit home, sharper glyph surfaces, mobile dashboard, always-visible chat composer, files, quick command deck, OS Intelligence panel, action-enabled push notifications, action ledger review, ClassOps dashboard, recovery health, and notification controls.

capabilityRings.name: Security
capabilityRings.description: Telegram fail-closed allowlist, web API token throttling, constant-time auth checks, safer rate-limit keys, /forget confirmation, file size guards, Redis job locks, and clearer saved/not-saved storage failure states.

capabilityRings.name: Context
capabilityRings.description: Timetable, Google Calendar, reminder-worthy events, rough travel buffers, MTL classlists and marks fields, Dropbox lesson folders, checked availability slots, Drive references, file memory, Postgres-backed long-term memory, topic profiles, conversation summaries, relevant-memory retrieval, intent lensing, wordsmith semantic profiling, short-lived working memory, and home intelligence synthesis.

capabilityRings.name: Live Knowledge
capabilityRings.description: Weather, MUIS, dual personal Gmail inbox support, experimental Work Gmail OAuth, Work Gmail action monitoring with runtime health, Google News, Tavily/search fallbacks, bounded X/Twitter-style social leads for digest topics, OpenAI-native web search hooks, fetch URL, source notes, live-check hints, structured Liverpool and F1 briefers.

capabilityRings.name: Self-Improvement
capabilityRings.description: Postgres-backed memory commits, priority-weighted memory prompt, relevant recall, working memory, conversation-continuation guardrails, OpenAI response-state continuity, semantic corpus tests, action validation/audits, persistent action ledger, source/memory/native-tool trace visibility, ClassOps blind-spot checks, intent lens, proactive workload insights, OS readiness scoring, forecast radar, adaptive planning, 7-day trial loop, notification feedback learning, implicit correction detection, weekly consolidation, and conversation summarisation.

chapters.title: Hira becomes installable
chapters.summary: H.I.R.A moved from a bot-shaped assistant toward a dedicated private app surface.
details: Installable PWA introduced.
Railway start mode added for the PWA.
Agenda, chat formatting, grouped school calendar variables, and task surfaces became part of the app shell.
chapters.impact: Anyone could now understand H.I.R.A as a personal cockpit, not just a Telegram command bot.

chapters.title: The app gets a body
chapters.summary: The PWA became visually and ergonomically distinctive: a cockpit, clock, notification controls, and branded H.I.R.A core.
details: Mobile cockpit and home branding polished.
Notification states, clock, asset refresh, and installed-app behavior were refined.
Chat streaming and backend responsiveness improved.
chapters.impact: H.I.R.A started feeling like a real daily-use system rather than a prototype page.

chapters.title: Hira learns to nudge, follow up, and carry files
chapters.summary: The assistant gained more practical work surfaces: attachments, Gmail-informed follow-ups, nudges in chat, and push reminders.
details: Chat attachment uploads added.
Gmail details began powering email follow-ups.
Push reminders bridged into the PWA chat.
Railway memory usage and overnight jobs were hardened.
chapters.impact: H.I.R.A became less passive: it could hold pending work and push useful reminders back into the app.

chapters.title: The daily workload dashboard takes shape
chapters.summary: H.I.R.A started showing workload state instead of only answering direct questions.
details: NEA weather arrived.
Marking load dashboards became class/work-type aware.
Daily score, workload trend, voice graph, signal module, and mobile dashboard states were refined.
Weather routing and header alignment improved.
chapters.impact: The home screen became a scan-friendly operational view of Herwanto's day.

chapters.title: Hira becomes more careful with facts
chapters.summary: The assistant gained stronger source discipline for prayer, location, schedule, and school data.
details: MUIS prayer and khutbah behavior became source-grounded.
PWA memory guardrails and notification worker mode improved stability.
Async uploads and notification health tools were added.
MTL Drive, classlist context, score entry, and analysis tools arrived.
chapters.impact: H.I.R.A became safer to trust for high-friction daily details: prayers, classlists, files, and score workflows.

chapters.title: Sports and browsing stop being guesswork
chapters.summary: H.I.R.A learned to read links, respect current Liverpool/F1 context, and avoid stale sports corrections.
details: Direct URL fetching added.
F1 and Liverpool preferences became first-class context.
Liverpool quick-route failure was fixed.
Structured Liverpool and F1 brief tools were added.
chapters.impact: When Herwanto asks about current teams, line-ups, results, or rumours, H.I.R.A now knows to verify instead of bluffing.

chapters.title: Hobby-plan architecture hardening
chapters.summary: The backend was tuned for Railway Hobby and made less fragile under real use.
details: Chat/upload concurrency increased responsibly.
Upload jobs moved into a bounded queue.
Web scheduling separated from the worker service.
Redis can be required in production.
Admin status endpoint exposes runtime, queues, models, notifications, and integrations.
chapters.impact: H.I.R.A gained a more reliable server shape: clearer ownership, better queues, and visible health.

chapters.title: Hira gets cleaner long-term memory
chapters.summary: Memory was split into clearer buckets, then upgraded with topic profiles for new interests.
details: Memory buckets added for teaching, business, sports, constraints, summaries, and topic profiles.
Model routing became configurable for quick, structured, agentic, and deep work.
Topic profiles now store what to track, preferred angle, live facts, stable context, and cadence.
chapters.impact: New interests can become structured knowledge lenses instead of scattered notes.

chapters.title: Hira gets a public growth story
chapters.summary: A dedicated minisite now charts H.I.R.A's growth from inception to current capability.
details: Growth story added at /growth and /hira-growth.
Timeline, capability rings, current capabilities, and update protocol are driven by JSON data.
Future upgrades can append a chapter to pwa/hira-growth-data.json.
chapters.impact: Anyone can now understand how much H.I.R.A has grown without reading the codebase or commit history.

chapters.title: Hira starts learning from corrections
chapters.summary: H.I.R.A gained a deterministic learning loop for corrections and meaningful chats.
details: Correction ledger added as high-priority memory.
Self-reflection notes now capture important learning moments from PWA and Telegram chats.
The system prompt now treats corrections and reflections as durable behaviour guidance.
chapters.impact: When Herwanto corrects H.I.R.A or introduces an important direction, that lesson can persist and shape future answers.

chapters.title: Browsing starts compounding into memory
chapters.summary: H.I.R.A gained a source-backed intake tool so useful research can become durable context instead of one-off answers.
details: New source_notes memory bucket added.
remember_source_insight stores topic, source, URL, insight, durability, and confidence.
Web, link, news, Liverpool, and F1 prompts now expose the source-note tool.
chapters.impact: When H.I.R.A researches a new topic, it can keep stable knowledge while marking volatile facts for future live checks.

chapters.title: Hira learns when not to trust memory alone
chapters.summary: The assistant gained source-discipline hints and a memory review endpoint.
details: Volatile questions now get an internal live-source requirement before the model answers.
Recommended tools are attached for sports, F1, Liverpool, weather, prayer, news, and pasted links.
Admin memory review exposes bucket counts, recent entries, and pruning rules at /api/admin/memory.
chapters.impact: H.I.R.A is now less likely to treat stale memory as truth, and its growing memory can be inspected before it gets messy.

chapters.title: Security hardened. Learning loop closed.
chapters.summary: A full code audit closed two critical security gaps and activated four intelligence upgrades that were structurally broken by design.
details: Telegram bot now enforces a user whitelist via HIRA_ALLOWED_USER_IDS — unauthorized users are rejected silently.
Web API now fails closed: if HIRA_WEB_TOKEN is not set, all endpoints return 503 instead of being publicly accessible.
/forget all now requires a second confirmation within 60 seconds to prevent accidental memory wipes.
Memory priority weighting fixed: correction_ledger now shows up to 20 items in the system prompt (was 8), constraints show all, low-signal buckets trimmed.
Weekly memory consolidation job added (Friday 22:30 SGT): Claude reviews corrections and reflections, promotes durable rules into preferences/constraints, and logs a growth note.
Conversation summarisation added: each session is summarised into recent_summaries on /clear or when the history window rolls over — giving H.I.R.A cross-session continuity.
Implicit correction detection added: after each reply, the router model checks if the user is contradicting a specific fact from the previous response, even without explicit correction keywords.
chapters.impact: H.I.R.A's learning loop is now fully closed: corrections are captured implicitly and explicitly, consolidated weekly into durable rules, and injected back into context with appropriate priority. The bot can no longer be accessed by strangers.

chapters.title: Security boundaries fail closed by default
chapters.summary: A follow-up review closed the remaining soft edges around Telegram access, web-token guessing, rate-limit identity, notification rendering, and public diagnostics.
details: Telegram startup now fails closed unless HIRA_ALLOWED_USER_IDS is set, with HIRA_TELEGRAM_OPEN_DEV_MODE reserved for explicit local development.
PWA API requests now reject invalid web tokens in middleware with a dedicated auth-failure limiter and constant-time comparison.
Rate limits no longer trust X-Forwarded-For by default; proxy headers require explicit HIRA_TRUST_PROXY_HEADERS opt-in.
Notification rendering now escapes quote characters and whitelists notification kind classes to close attribute-injection risk.
Public /healthz now exposes only minimal status while detailed runtime diagnostics remain behind /api/admin/status.
chapters.impact: H.I.R.A now behaves more like a private operating system: missing security config stops access instead of opening it, abuse attempts are slowed earlier, and diagnostic detail stays behind the token.

chapters.title: Hira starts noticing before being asked
chapters.summary: H.I.R.A gained a first proactive intelligence layer plus an intent lens that retrieves relevant memory before answering.
details: New intent lens infers Herwanto's likely hat and intent, then attaches relevant memory from corrections, constraints, reflections, summaries, topic profiles, projects, and source notes.
PWA and Telegram chat now receive this memory-aware lens before source-discipline hints, improving continuity across conversations.
Quick PWA replies also receive the lens, so lightweight chat can still respect durable context without pretending to use tools.
Proactive intelligence scanner now checks workload, due tasks, marking load, follow-ups, quiet windows, and upcoming packed days.
Deduplicated proactive insights run in the PWA worker and Telegram job queue, with configurable interval and active hours.
chapters.impact: H.I.R.A can now connect past lessons to present requests and surface early heads-ups like packed-day task pressure or marking pile collisions without waiting for Herwanto to ask.

chapters.title: Hira stops guessing availability
chapters.summary: A scheduling correction tightened H.I.R.A's handling of appointments and after-school slot recommendations.
details: New find_available_training_slots tool checks timetable lessons and Google Calendar before suggesting after-school slots.
Slot planning now supports duration, time window, purpose, and avoid-keywords such as CCA or football.
Requests like best slots, free slots, after school, not during CCA day, and Sahibba training now force the checked availability tool.
Appointment/date questions such as HDB appointment dates now force calendar context instead of relying on memory or inference.
System rules now explicitly say to copy exact appointment dates from tool results and never shift dates by assumption.
chapters.impact: H.I.R.A is less likely to suggest a training slot on a CCA day or move an appointment by a day; calendar/timetable truth is now mandatory for availability answers.

chapters.title: Hira learns to rank, suppress, and curate
chapters.summary: H.I.R.A's proactive system moved from separate notification jobs into one shared ranking pipeline, and the digest became a curated live shortlist instead of a flat headline dump.
details: Notification outcomes now persist as queue, push, seen, dismissed, useful, and not_now events.

A unified candidate -> score -> suppress -> send -> learn pipeline now ranks nudges, check-ins, follow-ups, prayer prompts, weekly planning, Friday review, proactive intelligence, tasks, and digest picks together.
Low-value repeat signals are cooled down automatically after dismiss or not_now feedback.
The home screen now surfaces a shared Priority Queue so the app and notification engine reflect the same top-ranked proactive items.
Morning digest now gathers multiple live Google News candidates per topic, scores them for quality and personal relevance, keeps topic diversity, and adds a short Why it matters layer.
chapters.impact: H.I.R.A is now less noisy, more selective, and more editorial. It can pull live inputs, rank what matters now, suppress stale or unwanted repeats, and present the best signals in a more curated daily flow.

chapters.title: Hira's notifications become recoverable and actionable
chapters.summary: H.I.R.A's push system was hardened so daily briefings, calendar/task reminders, and notification actions share a more reliable delivery and feedback loop.
details: Evening roundup delivery now mirrors morning briefing behavior: it only marks the day complete after confirmed phone push and can retry during a catch-up window.
Calendar/task reminders now use the proactive queue, including rough time-to-leave pushes for off-site-looking events without requiring paid routing APIs.
Reminder confidence levels classify signals as must_remind, probably_remind, digest_only, or ignore before they are allowed to push.
Notification health now exposes recovery status: last attempt, last success, recent misses, queued count, and likely blocking issue.
Push notifications gained action buttons: reminders can be marked done or snoozed, while updates/digests can be rated useful or not useful.
chapters.impact: H.I.R.A's notifications are now less fire-and-forget. They can retry, explain failures, learn from feedback, and accept lightweight user action straight from the push surface.

chapters.title: Hira stops losing the thread
chapters.summary: H.I.R.A gained a short-term working memory and action validation layer after a real Rhino/Sahibba follow-up drift exposed weak pronoun grounding.
details: PWA chat now keeps short-lived working memory per client: current subject, latest correction, pending action, and older competing subjects.
Vague follow-ups such as it, that day, and remind me again now receive recent-turn grounding before the model answers.
The chat can show an inline understanding cue such as Tracking Rhino Emergency Exercise and Action Morning Briefing Reminder.
State-changing tools now validate calendar events, reminders, nudges, check-ins, and follow-ups before saving.
Saved or blocked actions include internal action audits so reviewers can inspect what H.I.R.A thought it was doing.
Redis production guardrail warnings now appear in admin status when deployment state could fall back to one-process memory.
chapters.impact: H.I.R.A became safer for natural follow-up language: newer user corrections now beat older assistant guesses, and vague actions are blocked before they can save the wrong thing.

chapters.title: A premium home screen experiment is tested
chapters.summary: The PWA home screen was redesigned around a premium Living Daily OS concept while preserving H.I.R.A's Nothing-style dotted headings and numbers, then judged too heavy in the installed mobile app.
details: Home now opens with a premium greeting, live day subtitle, and compact command controls.
The main surface now prioritises Top 3 priorities, Living Timeline, next best action, protected time, and today's score.
The side rail now carries Daily Load, Risk Radar, Calendar Pressure, and Prayer/Window context.
Lower operations drawers keep Digest, Tasks, System Status, Marking Load, and File Memory available without dominating the first scan.
Light and dark palettes were upgraded toward ivory/graphite premium software while retaining dotted H.I.R.A display typography.
PWA app version moved to 20260508-2 with service worker cache hira-os-v70 for field testing.
chapters.impact: This became a useful design test rather than the final direction: the mobile app exposed oversized panels and poor bottom behavior, so the previous look was restored.

chapters.title: The previous cockpit look is restored
chapters.summary: After real phone screenshots showed the premium redesign did not translate well inside the installed app, H.I.R.A returned to the earlier cockpit-style interface.
details: The premium Daily OS layout was reverted without removing the working-memory and trust-rail upgrades.
The previous topbar, local time block, tab rail, dashboard sections, and chat layout were restored.
The PWA app version moved to 20260508-3 with service worker cache hira-os-v71 so installed devices receive the restored look as a fresh update.
The growth log now marks the premium redesign as an experiment, not the current interface.
chapters.impact: H.I.R.A should look familiar again in the app while keeping the newer reliability behavior underneath.

chapters.title: The home-screen icon joins the Nothing-style system
chapters.summary: H.I.R.A's installable identity and growth log were pulled into the same sharper visual language as the current app surface.
details: The app icon was rebuilt around the warm device plate, black cockpit core, LED-style H mark, red signal block, and rail/grid details used by the latest interface.
PNG home-screen assets were added alongside the SVG so saved app icons render cleanly across install surfaces.
The growth log palette moved from the older soft ivory/red treatment to the current Nothing-inspired off-white, graphite, grid, rail, and signal-red system.
Growth-log typography and panels were tightened: less soft-card language, stronger rails, clearer stage language, and cache-busted assets for the update.
chapters.impact: H.I.R.A now reads as one product from the phone home screen to the growth record: private, mechanical, alive, and recognisably tied to the current cockpit UI.

chapters.title: Hira becomes a daily command system
chapters.summary: H.I.R.A gained a visible intelligence stack for a one-week real-life trial: quick commands, readiness scoring, command protocol, forecast radar, adaptive planning, and a trial loop.
details: Home chat now includes a quick command deck for Brief Me, Triage, Marking Sprint, Draft Reply, and Reminder.
The backend now synthesizes agenda, workload, tasks, marking, proactive queue, service health, and file context into one OS Intelligence object.
The home screen now shows readiness, operating mode, next best move, risk signals, openings, and one-tap actions.
Command Protocol converts the diagnosis into Now, Next, and Later steps with confidence and evidence.
Forecast Radar predicts pressure from future load spikes, due clusters, marking drag, or stable horizon signals.
Adaptive Plan turns the live state into Prime, Build, Slice, and Stabilize blocks where relevant.
The 7-Day Trial Loop adds checkpoints and a Log Trial Note action so Herwanto can judge whether H.I.R.A reduces decision friction in real use.
chapters.impact: H.I.R.A is now ready to be tested as a practical daily operating layer: it does not only show data, it interprets the day, suggests an execution protocol, predicts pressure, and asks for feedback to improve the next pass.

chapters.title: The app gets cleaner and more accountable
chapters.summary: A phone-screenshot pass tightened the parts Herwanto would immediately see before the one-week trial: icon identity, glyph sharpness, input behavior, and email account clarity.
details: The app icon was simplified into a cleaner Nothing-style badge with a dotted H mark, sharper geometry, and less visual clutter.
The black glyph display was rebuilt with crisper dots and less blur so the OS identity looks higher-resolution inside the PWA.
The chat composer now hides the browser's thin native caret when the block cursor is active, leaving only the intended block blink.
Personal Gmail 2 was added as a first-class inbox option for brief and draft flows in the PWA, Telegram commands, service status, and setup docs.
Growth-log and app cache versions were bumped together so installed devices can receive the visible polish as a clean update.
chapters.impact: H.I.R.A should feel cleaner on first launch, less glitchy while typing, and more honest about email: personal inboxes can be connected, while work/MOE Gmail requires a separate Workspace OAuth experiment.

chapters.title: Work Gmail is reopened as a controlled test
chapters.summary: H.I.R.A reopened Work/MOE Gmail as an explicit experiment after personal Gmail 2 proved the multi-account path could work.
details: Work Gmail is now available as an account option in the PWA brief and draft flows.
Telegram Gmail commands and tool routing can use account=work instead of blocking work/MOE requests outright.
Service status now reports work_gmail separately from personal Gmail and Personal Gmail 2.
Setup docs and .env.example now document GOOGLE_WORK_GMAIL_CLIENT_ID, GOOGLE_WORK_GMAIL_CLIENT_SECRET, and GOOGLE_WORK_GMAIL_REFRESH_TOKEN.
The Gmail OAuth helper can print a custom Railway variable name so work-token generation is less error-prone.
chapters.impact: H.I.R.A can now try Work/MOE Gmail if Google Workspace permits the OAuth flow, while still treating admin blocking as a likely external constraint.

chapters.title: Work Gmail starts watching for action
chapters.summary: After Work Gmail OAuth succeeded, H.I.R.A gained a background monitor that checks work email and surfaces only messages that look like they need Herwanto's attention.
details: The PWA notification worker now runs a Work Gmail monitor on a configurable interval.
First run seeds the current inbox so old mail does not flood the phone.
New Work Gmail messages are scored for action cues such as replies, deadlines, approvals, forms, meetings, submissions, questions, and urgent language.
Action-worthy messages create deduplicated app notifications with sender, subject, reason, preview, and a prompt to inspect Work Gmail in H.I.R.A.
Config flags allow the monitor to be disabled, slowed down, pointed at a different Gmail query, or made more/less selective.
chapters.impact: H.I.R.A can now act as an attention filter for work email: it watches quietly, skips low-signal messages, and interrupts only when the inbox appears to contain something actionable.

chapters.title: The chat entry point stops hiding
chapters.summary: A mobile layout fix keeps the H.I.R.A typing box visible even before any quick command is selected.
details: The empty-chat mobile grid no longer collapses the composer row to zero height.
The composer now sits directly under H.I.R.A Output, before the quick-command tiles, so the place to type is visually obvious.
The quick-command deck remains available underneath the input as an accelerator rather than the only apparent way to begin.
chapters.impact: On the Home screen, Herwanto can immediately see where to type instead of needing to tap Brief Me, Triage, Draft Reply, or Reminder first.

chapters.title: Work Gmail monitoring becomes calmer
chapters.summary: H.I.R.A's Work Gmail watcher shifted from a 5-minute default to a 30-minute default to reduce unnecessary background checks after work hours.
details: The default Work Gmail monitor interval is now 1800 seconds.
.env.example documents the calmer 30-minute cadence.
The README now states that Work Gmail monitoring defaults to 30 minutes while staying configurable.
chapters.impact: H.I.R.A still watches for actionable work email, but the trial setup is gentler on Gmail quota, Railway background activity, and after-hours attention.

chapters.title: Hira gains receipts, lesson extraction, and feed-forward teaching intelligence
chapters.summary: H.I.R.A moved beyond a dashboard into a more accountable personal intelligence app: actions now leave reviewable receipts, chat traces expose memory sources, and ClassOps can turn lesson materials, submissions, and marks into teacher-facing next steps.
details: A persistent action ledger now records state-changing saves from assistant tools and the PWA, with review and undo support for selected actions.
Chat traces now show relevant memory sources alongside live-source contracts so answers are easier to audit.
ClassOps now reads Dropbox lesson folders and Google Drive classlists/marks into one teacher dashboard.
Selected Dropbox lesson files can be extracted read-only when they are PDF, DOCX, or PPTX, then used to produce post-lesson reflection worksheets.

ClassOps now surfaces priority items, possible blind spots, student watchlists, per-student submission timelines, and marks-aware feed-forward groups such as reteach, practice, catch-up, and extension.
A dependency-free local check script now runs compile and unittest verification before shipping behavior changes.
chapters.impact: H.I.R.A is now more trustworthy and more teacher-native: it can show what it changed, explain which memory shaped an answer, and help convert actual lesson evidence plus student signals into the next teaching move.

chapters.title: Hira gets a Postgres spine
chapters.summary: H.I.R.A moved its durable brain out of Google Sheets and into Postgres, then gained clearer operational checks so memory, config, notification state, delivery logs, and Work Gmail health can be inspected instead of guessed.
details: A Postgres storage layer now owns assistant memory, memory logs, app config, push subscriptions, push delivery logs, and notification state, with Sheets kept as a temporary fallback and as a read-only external source for human-maintained data.
The existing Sheet memory was imported into Postgres once, preserving 57 memory items across profile, preferences, teaching, projects, corrections, reflections, source notes, and other buckets.
Memory writes now commit through Postgres first, so H.I.R.A only says something was remembered after the database save succeeds.
/memcheck now reports the Postgres truth, while /storagecheck and /digestcheck expose storage health, fallback status, notification state, and digest delivery evidence.
The PWA connection card now treats a revoked Work Gmail token as a real reconnect state, and successful Work Gmail fetches or drafts clear stale reconnect warnings.
chapters.impact: H.I.R.A now has a sturdier operational backbone: durable memory is no longer hostage to Sheets quota or formatting, delivery behavior has receipts, and integration health can say connected, reconnect, unavailable, or failed with less guesswork.

chapters.title: Hira starts chasing live topic signals
chapters.summary: H.I.R.A's live-news brain was tightened so Android-style current events route to source tools, selected topics stay merged with the built-in radar, and the digest can use bounded social-search leads for fast-moving interests.
details: PWA prompts about Android, Android 17, Google I/O, Gemini, Pixel, iOS, Apple, AI, Nothing, Liverpool, and F1 now force live news/source tools instead of falling through to memory-shaped answers.
The unsupported backend-diagnosis guardrail now returns user-facing wording rather than internal instruction text.

The morning digest now always merges configured watch topics with built-in radar topics for Liverpool, F1/Mercedes, AI tools, Codex/Claude/Gemini/Kimi, Teenage Engineering, Android, iOS, Islam, Singapore education, Nothing Products, and Nothing OS.
A capped social-search supplement can query X/Twitter-style indexed results for the first few high-interest live topics, treating social posts as leads rather than final confirmation.
.env.example and README now document the social-search controls so the breadth can be tuned without code changes.
chapters.impact: H.I.R.A should now be much harder to catch out on fast-moving personal-interest news: it knows Android and the selected topic radar need live checks, and the digest can pick up fresher early signals while still keeping source discipline.

chapters.title: Hira learns not to lose the thread
chapters.summary: After a real conversation bug exposed H.I.R.A jumping from calendar cleanup into an unrelated habit check-in, the chat router was hardened so short replies follow the newest assistant offer instead of stale shortcuts.
details: Bulk duplicate calendar cleanup was added so replicated calendar items can be removed in one pass while one copy is kept.
Check-in completion now requires explicit completion wording or a genuinely recent check-in prompt, preventing generic replies like yes pls from marking habits done.

Short follow-ups such as yes, do it, go ahead, same, and that one now inspect the latest assistant offer before quick routing, then force the matching tool path for calendar views, Gmail drafts, reminders, searches, nudges, or deletions.
Telegram and PWA now share the safer check-in affirmation logic instead of drifting apart across surfaces.
Stale pending actions in PWA working memory are cleared when a fresh unrelated thread begins.
Brief me on this no longer hijacks into the daily briefing shortcut when the user is asking about the current subject.
chapters.impact: H.I.R.A should feel less senile in active conversations: Postgres keeps durable memory, while these guardrails protect the immediate thread so confirmations and short replies continue the right task.

chapters.title: Hira moves its engine room to OpenAI
chapters.summary: H.I.R.A was migrated away from Anthropic billing into an OpenAI-first provider path, then hardened with deterministic provider visibility and calmer daily worship check-in completion.
details: The core model layer now supports HIRA_LLM_PROVIDER=openai, translates H.I.R.A's tool schema into OpenAI Responses API function calls, and routes chat, JSON helpers, vision/document analysis, quick replies, and memory consolidation through OpenAI when enabled.
Provider/model status questions are answered from runtime configuration instead of asking the model to guess its own engine.
OpenAI defaults now track the current flagship model for agentic and deep work, with mini routing kept for quick, router, and structured tasks.
Anthropic remains only as an optional fallback while production testing completes, so Herwanto can stop Anthropic token billing without losing a rollback path.
Daily selawat/istighfar check-ins now treat phrases like ok done alhamdulillah, dah baca, and sudah baca as true completion while guarding against negated replies such as not done yet.
Done actions on phone notifications archive sibling check-in notifications with the same source so push recovery does not resurrect old duplicates after a habit is completed for the day.
chapters.impact: H.I.R.A is no longer tied to Anthropic for its main intelligence loop and is more honest about its runtime, while daily worship nudges behave closer to Herwanto's intent: one fixed reminder, then peace for the rest of the day.

chapters.title: Hira gets a sharper brain and better memory in the moment
chapters.summary: H.I.R.A's OpenAI migration was deepened into a more native, safer assistant loop: it now keeps better conversation state, uses newer OpenAI Responses features, traces native tools, and blocks the old istighfar/selawat reminder loop entirely.
details: OpenAI Responses streaming now stores response IDs per chat thread, so H.I.R.A can continue from the right conversation instead of treating every turn as fresh.
A model policy router now chooses reasoning effort, verbosity, specialist mode, native tools, and deep/agentic routing from the user's actual request.
Specialist modes now change the system instructions for calendar actions, teaching, sports, research, email, artifacts, and memory work.

OpenAI-native web search, file search, and code interpreter hooks are available through environment flags, with Singapore location context for live searches and safer disabled-network defaults for code interpreter.
Structured document and slide generation now use strict JSON schema responses on OpenAI, reducing malformed artifact specs.
The PWA trace now shows model policy, thread state, response IDs, source contracts, OpenAI-native tool activity, and citations so failures are easier to inspect.
The provider-status bug was fixed so normal questions no longer get derailed by injected debug text like HIRA_LLM_PROVIDER=openai.
Blank Done-style answers and false no-access claims now trigger fallback checks instead of pretending the task was answered.
Istighfar and selawat reminders are now removed, archived where possible, and blocked from being created or pushed again.
Backend endpoints now prepare OpenAI Realtime voice sessions, giving H.I.R.A the plumbing for future live spoken conversation.
chapters.impact: For Herwanto, H.I.R.A should feel less forgetful, less helpless, and more honest: it can keep the thread, check live topics properly, show its work in the trace, stop unwanted devotional reminders, and grow toward proper voice without pretending the voice layer is finished.

chapters.title: Hira learns Herwanto's wordsmith layer
chapters.summary: After a simple 'mark it pls' request exposed brittle language understanding, H.I.R.A gained a stronger semantic layer for indirect requests, reference-only nuance, low-priority cues, and clarification-first action safety.
details: Soft phrasing such as might be worth, no rush, for the file, park this, put a pin in, keep this on my radar, ring-fence, and learn my phrasing now maps to explicit semantic flags.
A confidence-scored semantic profile now distinguishes action, reference-only context, low priority, live-source research, memory, calendar, reminders, Gmail, artifacts, and follow-up intent.
Reference material only and no immediate action now suppress task creation unless Herwanto also gives a clear explicit action.
Not urgent and no rush now become low-priority action hints rather than being mistaken for no action.
Blocked calendar/reminder/follow-up actions now include richer Action audit context, and short replies such as all day can continue the pending clarification instead of starting a fresh turn.
PWA working memory now learns from blocked Action audits and named-thing references like the Perth thing so follow-up messages stay grounded in the current subject.
All-day and multi-day calendar events are now first-class, so leave, vacation, travel, and blocked whole-day ranges can be saved without inventing a time.
A wordsmith semantic corpus was added to tests so future routing changes have examples of Herwanto's actual phrasing to preserve.
chapters.impact: H.I.R.A should feel less literal and less fragile: it can infer the practical meaning behind Herwanto's language, hold ambiguous actions open, ask for the missing detail, and avoid turning reference-only notes into unwanted tasks.

currentCapabilities: Installable private PWA with minimal Nothing-style home-screen icon, sharper glyph surfaces, always-visible chat composer, chat, agenda, tasks, dual personal Gmail options, experimental Work Gmail, Work Gmail action monitoring, files, uploads, notifications, and dashboard views.

Home OS Intelligence that synthesizes agenda, workload, tasks, marking load, proactive signals, service health, and file memory into a readiness score, operating mode, next best move, risks, openings, and one-tap actions.
Persistent action ledger with review and selected undo support for assistant/PWA writes, plus chat trace visibility for relevant memory sources.

Postgres-backed durable storage for assistant memory, memory logs, app config, push subscriptions, push delivery logs, and notification state, with Sheets demoted to fallback and read-only external sources.
Conversation-continuation guardrails that route short replies like yes pls, do it, go ahead, same, and that one through the newest relevant assistant offer instead of stale shortcuts.

Wordsmith semantic layer that recognizes indirect requests, low-priority cues, reference-only notes, live-source phrasing, memory cues, artifact requests, and follow-up language from Herwanto's actual wording.

ClassOps teacher dashboard that combines Dropbox lesson folders, Google Drive classlists and marks, submission tracking, priority items, blind-spot checks, student watchlists, student timelines, and feed-forward groups.
Lesson-aware reflection worksheet generation from selected Dropbox PDF/DOCX/PPTX lesson files using read-only extraction.
Command Protocol that turns the current state into concrete Now, Next, and Later steps with confidence and evidence.
Forecast Radar that highlights upcoming pressure from load spikes, due clusters, marking drag, or stable horizon signals.
Adaptive Plan that creates live Prime, Build, Slice, and Stabilize work blocks from the current day state.
7-Day Trial Loop with checkpoints and a trial-note action to measure whether H.I.R.A reduces decision friction in real life.

Telegram-compatible tool brain with calendar, reminders, marking, MTL classlists, dual personal Gmail brief/draft support, experimental Work Gmail brief/draft support, work-email action monitoring, artifacts, weather, MUIS prayer/khutbah, live selected-topic news, social-search digest leads, web search, and URL reading.
Structured sports intelligence for Liverpool and F1.
Railway Hobby runtime profile with upload queue, memory guardrails, worker split, Redis production option, admin status, /memcheck, /storagecheck, and /digestcheck.
Postgres-backed long-term memory buckets, topic profiles for new interests, correction ledger, self-reflection notes, source-backed research notes, and conversation summaries.
Intent lens with relevant-memory retrieval before answers, including quick PWA replies.
Proactive intelligence scanner for workload pinch points, marking pressure, follow-ups, quiet windows, and upcoming packed days.
Unified Proactive Intelligence v2 queue that ranks nudges, check-ins, follow-ups, prayers, tasks, digest picks, and internal intelligence with suppression and feedback learning.
Short-lived PWA working memory that tracks the current subject, latest correction, pending action, and older competing subjects during follow-up chats.
Clarification memory that keeps blocked Action audit context alive so short replies like all day can continue the original calendar/reminder action instead of restarting the conversation.
Action validation and audit receipts for state-changing tools so vague calendar/reminder/nudge/follow-up saves are blocked or reviewable.
Restored cockpit-style home surface with local time, tabs, dashboard panels, workload context, marking, file memory, chat, refreshed identity assets, and preserved dotted H.I.R.A display typography.
Recoverable push notification system with Postgres delivery logs, notification state, recovery status, confirmed-push completion checks, and catch-up retry behavior for daily briefings.
Action-enabled push notifications: Done, Snooze 30m, Useful, and Not useful feed back into task/check-in/follow-up completion, proactive nudges, and notification outcome memory.
Calendar and task reminder push layer with reminder confidence levels and rough time-to-leave estimates from explicit notes, place overrides, or default travel buffers.

Curated live digest that merges configured watch topics with built-in radar topics, gathers Google News plus bounded X/Twitter-style social leads, ranks candidates for quality and relevance, and adds a short why-it-matters layer.
Checked availability planner for after-school training slots that combines timetable lessons, calendar conflicts, and CCA-day avoidance.
Priority-weighted memory prompt: corrections and constraints always visible; low-signal buckets trimmed.
Closed learning loop: implicit + explicit correction capture → weekly consolidation job → promoted to preferences/constraints.
Security: Telegram fail-closed allowlist, web API fail-closed auth, invalid-token throttling, safer notification rendering, /forget confirmation gate, and file upload size guards.
OpenAI-first model routing via the Responses API for quick replies, structured parsing, agentic work, and deep work, with Anthropic retained only as an optional fallback.
OpenAI response-state continuity, prompt caching, specialist instruction modes, native tool tracing, strict structured outputs, and realtime voice session groundwork.
Istighfar and selawat reminder suppression that removes matching check-ins, archives queued notifications where storage allows, and blocks future creation/delivery.
updateProtocol: Append a new chapter to pwa/hira-growth-data.json for every meaningful H.I.R.A upgrade.
Update summary.commitsTracked when major commits are added.
Adjust capabilityRings only when an upgrade changes a real capability, not for copy/UI polish.
Keep live facts out of permanent story text unless they are stable architecture facts.
